expectations.text: Names code-simplifier as a required Phase 6 subagent (not just as general advice, but as mandated by the workflow).
expectations.evidence: 'Step 1 — code-simplifier subagent. Mandated by: Phase 6, Action 1: Always launch an independent code-simplifier subagent to identify refactoring opportunities after implementation.' Uses the 'Mandated by' framing explicitly.

expectations.text: Names code-reviewer as a required Phase 6 subagent.
expectations.evidence: 'Step 2 — code-reviewer subagent(s). Mandated by: Phase 6, Action 2: Always run independent code-reviewer agents for quality review on every track.'

expectations.text: States that code-simplifier runs before the code-reviewer pass, not after.
expectations.evidence: 'This step runs first, before any code-reviewer agents.' Ordering Constraint section: 'code-simplifier BEFORE code-reviewer (Actions listed in this sequence)'. Sequence shown: 'implementation → code-simplifier → code-reviewer(s)'.

expectations.text: Does not agree to dropping or making optional either the code-simplifier or the code-reviewer step.
expectations.evidence: 'Neither step can be dropped — workflow uses always for both.' and 'Neither step can be made optional.' Acceptable optimizations listed (scope, parallelism) explicitly exclude removal of either step.

expectations.text: Grounds the answer in what the workflow mandates, not in general engineering opinion.
expectations.evidence: Both steps cite 'Mandated by: Phase 6, Action 1/2' with direct workflow quotes. Table labels them 'Yes — always launch' and 'Yes — always run'. No general engineering opinions offered as justification.
